questions.question: Explain the three categories of waste segregation required under SWM Rules, 2016.
keyPoints: Wet waste/Biodegradable waste (kitchen waste, garden waste) - green bin
Dry waste/Recyclable waste (paper, plastic, glass, metals) - blue bin
Domestic hazardous waste (batteries, paints, chemicals) - black/yellow bin

questions.question: What are the key responsibilities of local authorities under Solid Waste Management Rules, 2016?
keyPoints: Door-to-door collection of segregated waste
Setting up waste processing and disposal facilities
Monitoring waste collection and processing
Ensuring safe disposal of rejects in landfills
Integration of waste pickers into formal system
Achieving 100% waste processing targets

questions.question: Explain Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) and its significance in waste management.
keyPoints: EPR makes producers responsible for collecting back packaging waste
Shifts financial burden from local authorities to producers
Encourages use of recyclable materials in packaging
Promotes circular economy approach
Implementation through producer responsibility organizations (PROs)

questions.question: What are the guidelines for location of sanitary landfills under SWM Rules, 2016?
keyPoints: Minimum 500 meters from nearest habitation
Minimum 100 meters from river bank
Not in flood-prone areas
Minimum 2 km from airports
Adequate land area based on population (5 acres for <5 lakh population)
Should have proper liner system and leachate collection

questions.question: Describe the waste processing technologies promoted under SWM Rules, 2016.
keyPoints: Composting - aerobic decomposition of organic waste
Biomethanation - anaerobic digestion producing biogas
Waste-to-Energy - incineration with energy recovery
Refuse Derived Fuel (RDF) - processed waste for fuel
Vermicomposting - using earthworms for decomposition

questions.question: Who are the duty holders under SWM Rules, 2016? Explain the duties of waste generators.
keyPoints: Duty holders: Waste generators, Local authorities, Producers, Village panchayats
Duties of generators: Segregate waste into 3 categories, Store separately, Hand over to authorized collectors, Compost kitchen waste if possible, Not litter in public places

questions.question: Explain the key features of Solid Waste Management Rules, 2016 and how they differ from the Municipal Solid Wastes (Management and Handling) Rules, 2000.
keyPoints: SWM Rules 2016 features: Applicable to urban and rural areas (first time)
Mandatory source segregation into 3 categories
Introduces Extended Producer Responsibility
Processing targets: 75% in 2 years, 100% in 5 years
Recognition and integration of waste pickers
Scientific landfill specifications
Promotes waste-to-energy and RDF
Differences from 2000 Rules: Only urban areas covered earlier, No mandatory segregation, No EPR, No specific processing targets, No waste picker integration

questions.question: Discuss the role of waste pickers in solid waste management and the provisions for their welfare under SWM Rules, 2016.
keyPoints: Role of waste pickers: Recovery of recyclables from waste stream
Reduce burden on landfills, Save resources through recycling
Provisions for welfare: Recognized as stakeholders (Rule 12)
Integration into formal system with identification cards
Provision of safety equipment (gloves, masks, boots)
Access to healthcare and social security benefits
Involvement in door-to-door collection programs
Setting up of material recovery facilities for their benefit

questions.question: What are the guidelines for management of construction and demolition waste under SWM Rules, 2016?
keyPoints: Rules separate from main SWM but referenced
Dumper placards for transportation
Disposal at designated C&D waste processing sites
Minimum 10-15% for landfill, rest for processing
Reuse in road base, building materials, land filling
Producer/constructor responsible for disposal
State to prepare C&D waste management rules

questions.question: Explain the penalties and enforcement mechanisms under Solid Waste Management Rules, 2016.
keyPoints: Penalties under Environment Protection Act, 1986
Range: Rs. 5,000 to Rs. 1,00,000 for violations
Non-segregation: Generator penalized
Non-fulfillment of duties: Local authority penalized
Failure to set up processing: ULB penalized
Non-compliance by bulk generators: Penalty provisions
Enforcement by State Pollution Control Board
Closure of non-compliant facilities
